title: SQL: When NULL is in a column,it shouldn't add primary key
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE B (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, test INTEGER, name VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO B (test, name) VALUES (NULL, 'Test Name');
INSERT INTO B (test, name) VALUES (1, 'Another Name');
CREATE TABLE B_temp AS SELECT DISTINCT test, id FROM B WHERE test IS NOT NULL;
INSERT INTO B (test, name) SELECT test, name FROM B_temp;
key_bug_features: NULL values in primary key column
